original_query: select sum(l_extendedprice) / 7.0 as avg_yearly from lineitem, part where p_partkey = l_partkey and p_brand = 'Brand#35' and p_container = 'LG PKG' and l_quantity < ( select 0.2 * avg(l_quantity) from lineitem where l_partkey = p_partkey );
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_parts AS (
    SELECT p_partkey
    FROM part
    WHERE p_brand = 'Brand#35'
      AND p_container = 'LG PKG'
),
part_avg AS (
    SELECT l.l_partkey, 0.2 * AVG(l.l_quantity) AS avg_quantity
    FROM lineitem l
    JOIN filtered_parts fp ON l.l_partkey = fp.p_partkey
    GROUP BY l.l_partkey
)
SELECT SUM(l.l_extendedprice) / 7.0 AS avg_yearly
FROM lineitem l
JOIN filtered_parts fp ON l.l_partkey = fp.p_partkey
JOIN part_avg pa ON l.l_partkey = pa.l_partkey
WHERE l.l_quantity < pa.avg_quantity;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace the correlated subquery with a precomputed CTE that calculates the average quantity threshold for each part
Convert the implicit join in the WHERE clause to an explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and optimization
Precompute the 0.2 * AVG(l_quantity) value once per part instead of calculating it for each row
